data.title: Roche acquiring 89bio for $2.4B in push for liver & cardiometabolic therapies, offering $14.50 per share.
data.description: Roche Holdings AG has recently announced its acquisition of 89bio, Inc. for a substantial $2.4 billion deal. 89Bio is known as a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company with a primary focus on developing and marketing treatments for liver and cardiometabolic diseases. The acquisition will be finalized at $14.50 per share, representing a remarkable 79% premium over 89bio's closing stock price on September 17, 2025, which was the day before the deal was made public. This figure also marks a 52% premium over 89bio's 60-day volume-weighted average price. Moreover, as part of the agreement, 89bio stockholders will be granted a non-tradeable CVR to receive specific additional payments, totaling up to $6.00 per share.

data.title: Gold Reserve Ltd. updates on U.S. Court decision, Sept 18, 2025, 2020 Bondholders win partial summary judgment against PDVSA.
data.description: Gold Reserve Ltd. recently revealed that on September 18, 2025, the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York issued a ruling in favor of the 2020 Bondholders, rejecting Plaintiff PDVSA’s and PDVH’s motion for summary judgment. The court found the 2020 Notes, Indenture, Pledge Agreement, and Guaranty were validly issued. Gold Reserve Ltd. trades on the TSX.V under GRZ, BSX under GRZ.BH, and OTCQX under GDRZF.

data.title: Nym named to TIME's Top HealthTech Companies of 2025, "Outstanding" in AI & Data Analytics, among 400 companies from 25 countries.
data.description: Today, Nym, a top player in self-governing medical coding, was revealed as a recipient of the prestigious title of being among TIME’s World's Top HealthTech Companies of 2025. They were granted the highest rating of "Outstanding" in the AI & Data Analytics sector. This list, developed jointly with Statista, showcases 400 prominent HealthTech firms from 25+ nations over six key industry divisions. It spotlights the most groundbreaking organizations making waves in the healthcare sector.

data.title: Gold price drops over 1%; Darden earnings disappoint. Dow up 150+ points to 46,190.85; NASDAQ rises 1.07% to 22,500.28; S&P 500 jumps to 6,641.29.
data.description: U.S. stocks saw gains mid-day, with the Dow Jones index up over 150 points, reaching 46,190.85, a 0.37% increase. The NASDAQ rose 1.07% to 22,500.28, and the S&P 500 increased by 0.62% to 6,641.29. Information technology shares rose 0.8% while materials stocks fell by 0.7%. Darden Restaurants, Inc. (NYSE: DRI) reported first-quarter results below expectations, with earnings of $1.97 per share missing the estimated $2.00, and sales of $3.004 billion missing the forecasted $3.040 billion. 89bio, Inc. (NASDAQ: ETNB) shares surged 86% to $15.02 following news of an acquisition agreement. Further details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Global Trash Chute Market expected to reach USD 2.74 billion by 2034 with a 4.75% CAGR growth rate, driven by rapid urbanization and vertical construction, sustainability trends, and healthcare sector demands. Technology advancements and strict regulations are boosting market value.
data.description: The research report by Custom Market Insights highlights the Trash Chute Market, which was valued at around USD 1.85 Billion in 2024 and is expected to reach USD 1.93 Billion in 2025, projected to grow to approximately USD 2.74 Billion by 2034. The market is estimated to have a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of about 4.75% from 2025 to 2034. The growth is attributed to factors like rapid urbanization, high-rise constructions, sustainability trends, healthcare and hospitality sector expansions, and strict building regulations. Technological advancements in chute systems, including odor control, auto-cleaning, and smart monitoring, are also driving demand. The report covers various aspects such as market size, trends, insights by type, material, application/end-use, and region. Key regions covered include North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Middle East & Africa, and South & Central America. The report also emphasizes the importance of innovative chute designs to meet contemporary infrastructure requirements and improve hygiene standards. The increasing demand for sanitation and safe waste management, especially in healthcare settings, further boosts the market for chute systems.

data.title: Tezos (XTZ) price rises by 4.33% to $0.79 on strong bullish signals from MACD and neutral RSI, indicating potential upside.
data.description: Tezos (XTZ) has experienced a significant increase in price, reaching $0.79. This surge is supported by a bullish MACD momentum and a neutral RSI position, indicating the potential for further upward movement.

data.title: QIA and QC to take over Qikiqtarjuaq Port dev. from GN, aiming for Inuit-led joint venture.93% owned by QC. Design phase to finish soon.
data.description: Iqaluit, Nunavut, September 18, 2025 - Today, the Qikiqtani Inuit Association (QIA) and Qikiqtaaluk Corporation (QC) revealed intentions to take charge of the development of a sea port in Qikiqtarjuaq through an Inuit-led joint venture. The initiative by QC will utilize existing design work done by the Government of Nunavut (GN) to hasten the construction of this vital infrastructure in Nunavut. The project design phase of the Qikiqtarjuaq port, being advanced by the GN, is projected to be concluded by this fall. The joint venture, to be predominantly owned and operated by QC in collaboration with infrastructure partners, seeks to enhance the port's development.

data.title: PBIRx in Milford, Conn. celebrates Clinical Team growth - Andrew Reis named Director of Clinical Consultants with 20+ years of experience.
data.description: Milford, Connecticut - PBIRx, Inc., a prominent company in pharmacy benefit consulting and auditing, has made notable advancements in its Clinical Operations team. Andrew Reis, RPh, MBA, has been appointed as the Director of Clinical Consultants. Andrew, with a robust background of over 20 years in pharmacy benefit management acquired from positions at OptumRx, Catamaran, and NMHCRx, brings extensive industry insight and leadership skills to his new role. This appointment marks a significant milestone for PBIRx, underscoring the company's commitment to enhancing its clinical operations.

data.title: ElevenLabs & AILAS team up for voice ID system protecting actors from AI misuse, ensuring voice authorization & responsible AI usage.
data.description: ElevenLabs has collaborated with AILAS to launch a new voice ID system. This innovative technology enables actors to authorize the use of their voices by artificial intelligence. The main objective is to prevent misuse of voice recordings and encourage responsible adoption of AI. This partnership aims to address these concerns by providing a secure authorization process for actors.

data.title: OM price at $0.22 holds support, gains 5.79%, with neutral RSI and bullish MACD, near $0.23 resistance.
data.description: The price of OM hovers around $0.22, showing a daily increase of 5.79%. The relative strength index (RSI) is neutral, while the moving average convergence divergence (MACD) indicates a bullish trend. It is edging closer to the important resistance level of $0.23 on the Bollinger Band.

data.title: Meta stock rises after launching new AI-powered Ray-Bans, sparking optimism on Wall Street for future growth in smart glasses market.
data.description: Analysts specializing in Meta stocks anticipate that the company will play a pivotal role in expanding the market for AI-powered smart glasses. The unveiling of new Ray-Bans by Meta has led to a rise in their stock, with Wall Street projecting a promising outlook for the future of AI glasses.

data.title: Novo Nordisk stock surges as oral Wegovy leads to "significant" weight loss for patients.
data.description: On Thursday, Novo Nordisk's stock experienced a notable surge following positive results from its oral version of Wegovy, which led to significant weight loss in patients. The success of this new treatment caused a boost in Novo Nordisk's shares.
